hero.headline: Start Your Private Limited Company with Confidence
hero.subtext: Incorporate your business with ease and full compliance under the Companies Act, 2013 with Professionals Law’s expert assistance.

introduction.heading: Introduction & Compliance
body: A Private Limited Company is one of the most popular business structures in India, governed by the Companies Act, 2013 and regulated by the Ministry of Corporate Affairs (MCA).
It provides its shareholders with limited liability protection, it is a separate legal entity and is suitable for businesses looking to raise investment and grow.
These compliance's include filing of annual documentations, conduction of board meetings, maintenance of books as per the statutory requirements and following other corporate governance standards.

whyNeeded.heading: Why It Is Needed
body: A Private Limited Company Registration is what makes the most sense when you want to be taken seriously as a business, seek legal identity, credibility, scale-ability and limited liability. It enables businesses to court investors or grow operations and it is the passport they must present when applying for loans or government tenders.
Its dynamic and professional image make this layout suitable for new businesses and growing businesses.

body.title: Limited Liability
body.description: Shareholders are only liable to the extent of their shareholding. This means their personal assets are protected from the company’s debts or legal obligations.

body.title: Separate Legal Entity
body.description: A private limited company is recognized as a distinct legal entity. It can own property, open bank accounts, enter contracts and sue or be sued in its own name.

body.title: Credibility
body.description: Being a registered entity under the Companies Act boosts business reputation. It builds trust among customers, suppliers, lenders and potential investors.

body.title: Funding Access
body.description: Private limited companies are the preferred structure for raising capital from angel investors, venture capitalists and financial institutions. The ability to issue shares makes funding flexible and scalable.

body.title: Perpetual Succession
body.description: The company remains in existence regardless of changes in ownership or management. This ensures continuity and long-term business stability.

body.title: Tax Advantages
body.description: Private limited companies can benefit from various tax deductions and exemptions, especially under schemes like Startup India. This can significantly reduce the tax burden during early growth stages.

body.title: Transferability
body.description: Shares of a private limited company can be transferred easily among existing or new shareholders.  This allows for seamless changes in ownership without affecting the company’s operations.

criteria: Minimum of 2 directors (at least one must be a resident of India)
Minimum of 2 shareholders (can be the same as directors)
A registered office address in India
Unique company name not similar to existing companies or trademarks

documents: Proof of address (bank statement/electricity bill not older than 2 months)
Utility bill (electricity/gas/water not older than 2 months)

body.description: Apply for name reservation via SPICe+ service.
Obtain DSCs for all proposed directors and subscribers.
Assigned through SPICe+ form during incorporation.
Submission of SPICe+ along with e-MoA (Memorandum of Association) and e-AoA (Articles of Association).
MCA issues Certificate of Incorporation with CIN and PAN/TAN.

body.question: Can a single person register a Private Limited Company?
body.answer: No, a minimum of two shareholders and two directors is mandatory.

body.question: Is it necessary to have an office address for registration?
body.answer: Yes, a valid registered office address is required during incorporation.

body.question: Can NRIs or foreign nationals be directors/shareholders?
body.answer: Yes, they can be, subject to at least one Indian resident director.

body.question: What is the minimum capital required to start a private company?
body.answer: There is no minimum capital requirement. You can start with even ₹1.

body.question: Do I need to visit any government office for registration?
body.answer: No, the entire process is online through the MCA portal.

body.question: What is the validity of a Private Limited Company registration?
body.answer: It is valid for a lifetime, subject to regular compliance.

body.question: Can I convert my existing sole proprietorship to a Private Limited Company?
body.answer: Yes, the conversion is possible by following the MCA-prescribed procedure.

body.question: Is audit mandatory for a Private Limited Company?
body.answer: Yes, even if there is no turnover, a statutory audit is mandatory.

body.question: What are the annual compliance requirements?
body.answer: Annual return filing, financial statement submission, board meetings, ROC filings, tax return filing etc. are required.
